task_completion.description: Agent successfully logged into the FreeScout helpdesk using the provided credentials.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FreeScout dashboard or mailbox view is visible in the web browser, indicating a successful login as admin.

task_completion.description: Agent navigated to the Support mailbox to view the list of tickets.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Support mailbox ticket list is displayed, showing a mix of tickets from both the spam and legitimate senders.

task_completion.description: Agent identified and selected the spam tickets from the domain '@spam-lottery.xyz'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Checkboxes next to the tickets from 'winner@spam-lottery.xyz' are checked, or the agent is actively viewing/selecting these specific tickets.

task_completion.description: Agent moved all 5 spam tickets to the Trash.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Trash folder or deleted tickets view in FreeScout explicitly shows the 5 tickets from 'winner@spam-lottery.xyz'.

task_completion.description: Agent preserved the legitimate ticket from '@legit-lotto.com'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The active Support mailbox still contains the 1 ticket from 'account.manager@legit-lotto.com', and it is NOT present in the Trash folder.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the FreeScout web GUI rather than using terminal commands or direct database manipulation.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser with the FreeScout interface being clicked and navigated. No terminal windows with SQL commands or API scripts should be visible.

integrity.description: Agent performed actions on the actual FreeScout application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser URL bar shows a local address consistent with the FreeScout deployment, and the UI elements (menus, buttons, ticket lists) match standard FreeScout web application behavior.

integrity.description: Agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the final state.
integrity.visual_evidence: No browser developer console or 'Inspect Element' panels are open modifying the DOM to artificially show tickets in the Trash or active mailbox.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The application is FreeScout. There are exactly 5 spam tickets, all from the sender 'winner@spam-lottery.xyz' (domain: spam-lottery.xyz). There is exactly 1 legitimate ticket from the sender 'account.manager@legit-lotto.com' (domain: legit-lotto.com). The agent must delete the 5 spam tickets and keep the 1 legitimate ticket.
